'''Jamie Thackray''' (born 30 September 1979) is a former Great Britain international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward. He played for over a dozen professional clubs throughout his career, most notably appearing for [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Hull FC]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Workington Town]] and [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] 

==Biography==
===Early career===
Born in [[Leeds]], Thackray played junior [[rugby league]] for [[Oulton Raiders]] before turning professional with [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]],<ref>{{cite news |url=https://infoweb.newsbank.com/apps/news/openurl?ctx_ver=z39.88-2004&rft_id=info%3Asid/infoweb.newsbank.com&svc_dat=AWGLNB&req_dat=1054640702C8DBC0&rft_val_format=info%3Aofi/fmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Actx&rft_dat=document_id%3Anews%252F112E2F4D69EF5DE8 |title=Thackray: I'll fight for a starting place |work=Yorkshire Evening Post |date=14 July 2006 | via=[[NewsBank]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> where he made his debut in 1997. He appeared as a substitute in the club's 12–11 win over [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] in the [[1999 Northern Ford Premiership]] Grand Final.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Time runs out for Dewsbury |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-time-runs-out-for-dewsbury-1122450.html |website=The Independent |access-date=14 April 2024 |date=25 September 1999}}</ref>

===Super League career===
[[File:Jamie Thackray.jpg|thumb|left|Thackray playing for Hull FC]]
After a stint with [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], Thackray moved to [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Thackray joins Tigers |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/2336465.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=17 October 2002}}</ref> During these seasons, Thackray played some games as a {{rlp|sr}} before settling into his permanent role as a prop forward. His appearances at Castleford were limited due to suffering a broken arm twice during his two seasons at the club.<ref>{{cite web |title=Nowhere Man regains command of destiny as the Comeback Kid takes to home stage |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/nowhere-man-regains-command-of-destiny-as-the-comeback-kid-takes-to-home-stage-515999.html |website=The Independent |access-date=14 April 2024 |date=19 November 2005}}</ref>

After Castleford, Thackray moved to [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Hull seal Thackray signing |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/3682860.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=23 September 2004}}</ref> Thackray played for Hull in the [[2005 Challenge Cup]] Final from the interchange bench in their victory against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Hull stun Leeds in Challenge Cup|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/4169230.stm|access-date=17 August 2013|newspaper= [[BBC News]]|date=27 August 2005}}</ref> He was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] at the end of the season, and was selected by [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] for the [[2005 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2005 Tri-Nations]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Thackray props up Lions' hearts and minds for toughest test of all |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/nov/05/rugbyleague.gdnsport3 |website=The Guardian |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=5 November 2005}}</ref> winning three caps during the tournament.

He then moved to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 2006.<ref>{{cite web |title=Thackray makes early Leeds move |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leeds/5147812.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=7 July 2006}}</ref> In February 2007, he scored a memorable try in a 22–30 defeat against [[Catalans Dragons]] which won the club's try of the season award.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds Rhinos Nostalgia: Thackray's magic was highlight of Rhinos' defeat by the Dragons |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-nostalgia-thackrays-magic-was-highlight-of-rhinos-defeat-by-the-dragons-626099 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=14 April 2024 |date=22 February 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet | number=1249645756211961856 | title=Jamie Thackray | Leeds Rhinos' 2007 Try of the Season |user=TheRFL |author=Rugby Football League |author-link=Rugby Football League |access-date=14 April 2024}}</ref>

After a spell on loan to [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[National League Cup (rugby)|Northern Rail Cup]],<ref name="GB star joins Dons on loan">{{cite web|url=http://www.thestar.co.uk/doncaster-sport/GB-star-joins-Dons-on.3801493.jp|title=GB star joins Dons on loan|work=The Star|date=2008-02-21|access-date=2008-02-21}}</ref><ref name="Thackray set for Doncaster début">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/02/22/RUGBYL_Doncaster.html|title=Thackray set for Doncaster début|publisher=Sportinglife|date=2008-02-22|access-date=2008-02-22}}</ref> he re-joined Hull in March 2008.<ref name="Thackray rejoins former club Hull">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/7314345.stm|title=Thackray rejoins former club Hull|publisher=BBC|date=2008-03-27|access-date=2008-03-27}}</ref> He helped the club reach the [[2008 Challenge Cup]] final, but Hull received a fine of £100,000 as Thackray had made ineligible appearances in earlier rounds of the competition due to not being registered.<ref>{{cite web |title=Hull fined £100,000 |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/jul/08/hullfcrl.rugbyleague |website=The Guardian |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=8 July 2008}}</ref>

At the end of the 2009 season, Thackray was released by Hull. In early 2010, Thackray signed for [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]].<ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.express.co.uk/posts/view/151798/Crusaders-move-in-for-Jamie-Thackray| title = Crusaders move in for Jamie Thackray {{!}} Rugby League {{!}} Sport {{!}} Express.co.uk| date = 15 January 2010}}</ref> Within a fortnight, he made his début for Crusaders against his former team, Leeds and went on to appear 20 times for the Welsh outfit during their Super League campaign.

===Later career===
On 12 November 2010, it was announced that he had joined Super League hopefuls [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] for the 2011 Championship campaign.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/raiders/raiders-sign-gb-international-prop-1.779469?referrerPath=home |title=Raiders sign GB international prop |access-date=2010-11-12 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121005171708/http://www.nwemail.co.uk/raiders/raiders-sign-gb-international-prop-1.779469?referrerPath=home |archive-date=2012-10-05 }}</ref>

In 2012 he joined [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Workington sign ex-Great Britain prop Jamie Thackray |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16354988 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=29 December 2011}}</ref>

In 2014, Thackray played in Australia for [[Cairns District Rugby League]] club [[Atherton Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Innisfail tops Tully |url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2014/04/29/innisfail-tops-tully/ |website=Queensland Rugby League |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=29 April 2014}}</ref>

After a spell playing [[rugby union]] for [[Hullensians]], Thackray joined [[London Broncos]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Thackray returns to help out for Broncos |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/thackray-returns-to-help-out-for-broncos-1823084 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=6 March 2015}}</ref> In 2016, his 20th season as a professional rugby league player, he was awarded a testimonial by the [[Rugby Football League]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Thackray awarded testimonial by RFL |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/thackray-awarded-testimonial-by-rfl |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=14 April 2024 |date=10 May 2016}}</ref>

Thackray initially ended his playing career with [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in 2017, but came out of retirement to sign for [[Keighley Cougars]] in 2019.<ref>{{cite web |title=Cougars sign Jamie Thackray and Macauley Hallett in double swoop |url=https://www.keighleynews.co.uk/sport/17412543.cougars-sign-jamie-thackray-macauley-hallett-double-swoop/ |website=Keighley News |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=6 February 2019}}</ref> At the age of 40, he signed a one-year deal with [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] for the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Thackray does a deal with Haven |url=https://www.whitehavennews.co.uk/sport/17998167.thackray-deal-haven/ |website=Whitehaven News |access-date=13 April 2024 |date=28 October 2019}}</ref>

After retiring from rugby league, Thackray began running a caravan construction company.<ref>{{cite web |title=Jamie Thackray | Transition |url=https://rlplayersportal.co.uk/blog/jamie-thackeray-transition |website=Rugby League Player Portal |date=26 August 2022 |access-date=14 April 2024}}</ref>
